skill.name: higgsfield-generate
skill.description: Generate images and videos using Higgsfield AI models. Supports GPT Image 2, Seedance 2.0, Marketing Studio for ads, Soul models, and Virality Predictor for video scoring.
skill.summary: AI-powered image and video generation with 30+ models including Marketing Studio for branded ads.
skill.author: higgsfield-ai

security_audit.summary: All 849 static findings (external_commands, filesystem, network, weak crypto, pipe-to-shell, system reconnaissance) are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill documents legitimate `higgsfield` CLI commands inside markdown code blocks. The 'pipe to shell' at SKILL.md:39 is the standard official install command for the higgsfield CLI. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' hits are model version identifiers (e.g., gpt_image_2, seedance_2_0), not crypto references. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent found. Risk level is LOW because the skill does execute external CLI commands as its core function, which carries inherent minor risk.

low_findings.title: External CLI commands in documentation (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 756 instances of shell backtick execution patterns. These are all `higgsfield` CLI commands (e.g., `higgsfield generate create`, `higgsfield model list`) shown inside markdown code blocks as documentation. This is the intended use of the skill - driving the official Higgsfield CLI.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm references (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged model version identifiers (e.g., `gpt_image_2`, `seedance_2_0`, `soul_v2`) as weak cryptographic algorithm references. These are model name/version strings, not encryption algorithm references.

low_findings.title: Pipe to shell install pattern (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:39 contains `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/higgsfield-ai/cli/main/install.sh | sh`. This is the standard, official installation command for the `higgsfield` CLI tool from the official GitHub repository, not a malicious curl-to-sh pattern.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance commands (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged API discovery commands like `higgsfield model list`, `higgsfield marketing-studio avatars list` as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate API queries to discover available options, not host/network reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged URLs like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/higgsfield-ai/cli/main/install.sh` (official CLI installer), `https://shop.example.com/` (example/documentation URL), and placeholder URLs. These are not malicious endpoints.

low_findings.title: Temp file creation for CLI arguments (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged `mktemp` usage at SKILL.md:198-199 and marketing-avatars.md:34. These create temporary files to pass JSON arrays via `@/path/to/file.json` to the CLI. This is standard shell practice for passing structured data to CLI tools.

content.user_title: Generate professional images, videos, and ads with AI
content.value_statement: Creating visual content for marketing campaigns is slow and expensive. This skill generates professional images, videos, and branded ads using Higgsfield AI models - just describe what you want and get production-ready media.
actual_capabilities: Generate high-fidelity images using GPT Image 2, Nano Banana 2, and Soul models
Create videos from text prompts or images using Seedance 2.0, Kling 3.0, and Cinema Studio
Produce branded marketing ads with avatars, products, hooks, and settings via Marketing Studio
Analyze video virality potential with hook strength, attention, and retention scores
Import products from URLs or uploaded images for ad creation
Create custom avatars from uploaded photos for consistent presenter identity
limitations: Cannot train Soul Characters (use the higgsfield-soul-id skill for that)
Does not create product photoshoots or marketplace listing cards
Cannot process text, chat, or text-to-speech tasks
Requires the higgsfield CLI to be installed and authenticated

use_cases.title: Create a social media ad campaign
use_cases.description: A marketer generates a series of video ads for Instagram and TikTok using Marketing Studio with product images and a brand avatar.

use_cases.title: Analyze video creative performance
use_cases.description: A content creator uploads a finished video and uses Virality Predictor to get hook strength, retention, and attention scores before launching the campaign.

use_cases.title: Generate product demonstration videos
use_cases.description: An e-commerce business imports product URLs and creates unboxing or demo videos with custom avatars and branded settings.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a red sports car parked on a coastal road at sunset, cinematic lighting, 16:9 aspect ratio.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple image

prompt_templates.title: Video from an existing image
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this photo into a 10-second video with a slow dolly-in effect, gentle waves moving in the background, warm golden hour light.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to animate a static image

prompt_templates.title: Branded ad with product
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second UGC ad video featuring the coffee maker product with a friendly presenter avatar, using the energetic hook style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Marketing professional creating a product ad

prompt_templates.title: Video virality analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this video creative and tell me the hook strength, attention retention score, and overall virality potential with recommendations to improve.
prompt_templates.scenario: Content creator evaluating a finished video

output_examples.input: Generate a 15-second brand video ad for a new sneaker product with a sporty avatar.
output_examples.output: A 15-second vertical video showing the sneakers from multiple angles with an athletic presenter demonstrating the product against a city backdrop.

output_examples.input: Analyze the virality potential of this 30-second ad video.
output_examples.output: Overall score: 72/100. Peak hook at 3 seconds (85% attention). Strong visual cortex engagement. Sustained retention at 78%. Open the full report for detailed breakdown.

best_practices: Use concrete, sensory prompts that describe subject, setting, style, and camera motion for best results
Pass the --wait flag to generate commands for one-shot submission that blocks until completion
Chain with higgsfield-soul-id for consistent face and identity across multiple generations
anti_patterns: Do not pass both an ad reference video AND hook/setting blocks - these approaches are mutually exclusive
Do not pass --hook_id or --setting_id to marketing_studio_image (only marketing_studio_video supports them)
Do not redescribe the input image in the prompt for image-to-image tasks - describe only the changes

faq.question: What is the default model for image generation?
faq.answer: GPT Image 2 is the default for high-fidelity general images, design, and text. Nano Banana 2 is the default for character and cartoon work.

faq.question: What is the default model for video generation?
faq.answer: Seedance 2.0 is the default all-purpose video model for serious motion, cinematic clips, and image-to-video tasks.

faq.question: Can I use my own images as input?
faq.answer: Yes. Media flags accept both local file paths (auto-uploaded) and UUIDs (upload IDs or previous job IDs).

faq.question: How do I use a specific person's face across generations?
faq.answer: First train a Soul Character using the higgsfield-soul-id skill to get a reference ID. Then pass --soul-id to Soul 2.0 or Soul Cinema models.

faq.question: What is Marketing Studio?
faq.answer: Marketing Studio creates branded image and video ads using avatars, products, hooks, settings, and ad reference videos.

faq.question: What models does the skill support?
faq.answer: It supports over 30 models including GPT Image 2, Seedance 2.0, Nano Banana 2, Kling 3.0, Soul models, Cinema Studio, Marketing Studio, and Virality Predictor.
